threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stare blankly at the bus stops, waiting for a timetable that doesn’t exist. Locals have their routes memorized like a secret map.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week, and a guy tried to sell a can of Red Bull for a fiver. Must've thought we were tourists.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the lady in a polka dot dress arguing with a street musician over the volume of his accordion by the canal. Classic Neukölln moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a place where they serve Currywurst on a ping pong table. It's like dinner and a game all rolled into one.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their ability to seamlessly navigate around BART delays, while newcomers just stand there confused, checking their phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that took a 15-minute detour just to avoid a street performer playing 'Wonderwall' on loop. Classic San Francisco chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all the locals go to the same bars, but most of us have our secret spots, tucked away from the tourist trails.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still awkwardly navigate the escalators at metro stops—holding tight to the rail and looking around confused.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it the tube instead of the underground; locals have no idea what you mean when you say 'the Central Line.'
The 59 bus driver last night stopped for a pigeon trying to cross Coldharbour Road. He just sighed and let it waddle across!

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a kid on a scooter dodging a pigeon while his mom chatted with a neighbor over coffee outside the Spätkauf. Definitely home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find techno parties that start on a Monday morning and last until Tuesday's sunrise, with folks emerging from the U-Bahn like it's just another weekend.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they walk past the BART crowds without flinching, while the newcomers gawk and panic at the delays.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 at 6 PM yesterday, packed shoulder to shoulder. Someone got off at Market and forgot their burrito—hilarious chaos as it rolled down the steps.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every plaza is for leisure, but locals know that Plaça del Sol turns into a makeshift bar during the late nights. No quiet spots here!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their overly enthusiastic use of 'Hola!'—it's like every interaction is an audition for a friendly local role.
